author: Zhekai Chen, Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Bohao Li, Yuqing Wang, Manyuan Zhang, Xihui Liu

readme.entry: - **[UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08768)** — Zhekai Chen, Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Bohao Li, Yuqing Wang, Manyuan Zhang, Xihui Liu — <https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08768> · *paper/benchmark/code* — A 400-task bilingual benchmark for proactive agents that runs real browser, CLI, file, and GUI work in fresh containers with closed-loop user feedback and hidden checkpoint grading. 🆕

factuality.reason: structured evidence plus fetched source snapshot

quality.reason: concrete eval artifacts or methodology signals present

recency.reason: evidence indicates 2025-2026 or actively updated source

section: 9 · Agent-specific evaluation (trajectories, tool use, multi-turn, world state, multi-agent, localization)
title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks

worker.candidate_reason: It clears the bar with a capability-driven taxonomy, a reproducible Docker runtime, public tasks and artifacts, and an explicit three-role design that prevents the user simulator from leaking hidden rubrics while still testing recovery across turns.
worker.evidence: HF/arXiv paper and MIT-licensed HKU-MMLab/UniClawBench repo retrieved: 400 tasks (200 English, 200 Chinese) across five capabilities; fresh Docker execution; hidden supervisor returns pass/continue/fail; public user simulator sees only visible trajectory plus sanitized status; repository ships tasks, runtimes, traces, artifacts, and leaderboard tooling.
worker.summary: A 400-task bilingual benchmark for proactive agents that runs real browser, CLI, file, and GUI work in fresh containers with closed-loop user feedback and hidden checkpoint grading.
